pattern: Deciding which of two binary strings should go first is a local comparison -- put x before y whenever the number formed by x+y beats y+x -- so sorting the three binary strings by that pairwise rule (the same idea used to build the largest number from digit strings) gives the best global order.
hints: There are only 3 numbers, so their binary representations can be placed in any of 6 orders -- but you don't need to try all 6 by hand if you find the right pairwise rule.
For any two binary strings x and y, compare the two numbers you'd get from x+y and from y+x -- whichever is larger tells you which string should come first between just those two.
Sort all three binary strings using that pairwise comparison, concatenate them in the resulting order, and parse the result as a binary number.

edge_inputs.description: all three at the minimum value
all three at the maximum value
one very large value and two very small ones
binary lengths differ enough that naive string comparison could mis-order them

stress.description: random triples across the full value range
